story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that the ongoing crisis in West Asia is impacting global energy and shipping routes, prompting governments to take steps to support MSMEs and secure critical pathways. Analysts suggest India’s FY26 growth may face limited disruption, though rising oil prices could lead to higher costs. Additionally, inflation in India has risen to a 38-month high of 3.9% in March, driven by soaring energy and crude prices linked to the West Asia conflict.

story_clusters.summary: India is facing an energy squeeze as the U.S. ends waivers allowing it to import oil from Iran and Russia. Meanwhile, crude oil prices remain below $95 amid ongoing Iran-U.S. peace talks and hopes for Middle East de-escalation. The situation is further complicated by regional tensions, including concerns over the Hormuz Strait and potential currency wars.

The amendments modify the existing Branch Authorisation Directions under the Banking Financial Companies framework in India.

story_clusters.summary: Arvind Kejriwal has filed an affidavit seeking the recusal of Justice Swarana Sharma from his case, citing a "reasonable apprehension of conflict of interest" due to her children being employed as counsel for the Centre. Multiple reports confirm that Kejriwal’s plea alleges the judge’s children’s association with the government could influence the proceedings. Justice Swarana Sharma’s full name is Swarna Kanta Sharma, as noted by CNBC India.

articles.title: Section 14A Disallowance Restricted to Exempt-Income Investments; ESOP Expense Allowed
articles.summary: An Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that Section 14A disallowances must be limited to investments yielding exempt income and ordered recomputation under Rule 8D. The tribunal also allowed Employee Stock Option Plan (ESOP) expenses as a valid business deduction under Section 37(1), treating them as an ascertained liability rather than a notional or capital expense.

articles.title: No FTS on Project-Specific Design Services; ‘Make Available’ Test Not Met, No Disallowance u/s 40(a)(i)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that consultancy payments for architectural services do not qualify as 'Fees for Technical Services' (FTS) because no technical knowledge was 'made available.' As a result, the disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) of the Income Tax Act was deleted. The ruling clarifies tax treatment for project-specific design services in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that consultancy payments for architectural services were not FTS since no technical knowledge was made available. Disallowance under Section 40(a)(i) was deleted. The post No FTS on Project-Specific Design Services; ‘Make Available’ Test Not Met, No Disallowance u/s 40(a)(i) appeared first on TaxGuru .

This decision sets a legal precedent for banks' obligations in handling cheques, reinforcing consumer rights under Indian law.

articles.summary: The High Court examined whether an FIR filed under GST law without prior sanction is legally valid. It permitted the investigation to continue but restrained arrest and barred the filing of a charge sheet until further hearings. The ruling highlights the need for prior sanction under GST law before initiating such legal proceedings.

articles.title: SCN Invalid as Issued Under Wrong Provision for GTO Services, Demand Set Aside
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a service tax demand notice (SCN) issued under Section 73 for GTO services was invalid because recipients governed by Section 71A cannot be proceeded against under Section 73. The demand was set aside due to procedural invalidity. The ruling highlights the importance of correct legal provisions in tax enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved service tax demand on GTO services using Section 73. The Tribunal held that recipients governed by Section 71A cannot be proceeded against under Section 73. articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that differential Countervailing Duty (CVD) cannot be imposed on an importer without proof linking them to the alteration of Maximum Retail Price (MRP) labels. articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that differential CVD cannot be demanded without evidence linking the importer to MRP alteration. articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued guidelines for appointing an Amicus Curiae when a convict's counsel is absent. The Registry must first serve notice to the convict's address via the police station. If the convict remains unresponsive despite service, the Court may proceed to decide the appeal. This aims to prevent technical pleas of unfairness in legal proceedings.

articles.title: No reassessment beyond 3 years without proper sanction of prescribed higher authority
articles.summary: The article highlights a legal ruling that tax reassessment notices issued beyond three years are invalid without prior approval from the prescribed higher authority under Section 151(ii). The ruling specifies that approval by the Principal Commissioner alone is insufficient, emphasizing mandatory higher authority sanction. This impacts tax administration and reassessment procedures in India.
articles.raw_summary: Reassessment proceedings was invalid for a notice issued beyond three years without the sanction of the prescribed higher authority as prior approval must mandatorily be obtained from the authorities specified under Section 151(ii) and approval by the Principal Commissioner was not valid in such cases. The post No reassessment beyond 3 years without proper sanction of prescribed higher authority appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Karnataka HC Quashed GST Adjudication as Order Passed Without Hearing Taxpayer
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside a GST adjudication order because the tax authority passed it without giving the taxpayer an opportunity to be heard. The court emphasized the importance of fair procedures before finalizing tax demands. The petitioner has been allowed to respond to the show-cause notice in fresh proceedings. This ruling underscores the need for due process in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The Court emphasized the need for fair opportunity before finalizing tax demands. The petitioner was allowed to respond to the show-cause notice in fresh proceedings. articles.title: Access To Electricity A Fundamental Right U/Art 21: Allahabad HC Comes To Aid Of Daughter-In-Law Seeking Power Connection

The court held that such appeals cannot be cured or converted after the limitation period under Section 61(2) of the IBC expires. The ruling overturns the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal's (NCLAT) earlier approach on the matter.

articles.title: DRP 130426: CAG Reveals Governance Failure in J&K Wetlands Management
articles.summary: A CAG report reveals that the Jammu and Kashmir government's conservation efforts for wetlands were limited to just six lakes, while 691 other lakes were ignored. The Forest Department did not identify eligible water bodies or seek assistance under central schemes. This raises concerns about governance and environmental policy implementation in the region.
articles.raw_summary: According to the report, government conservation efforts were limited to only six lakes—Dal, Wular, Hokersar, Manasbal, Surinsar, and Mansar—which were examined in detail. For the remaining 691 lakes, the Forest Department neither identified eligible water bodies nor formulated plans to seek assistance under schemes of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF CC).

articles.title: Unexplained Credits Taxed u/s 68: No Business Income or 80-IB Deduction Without Proof
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that unexplained credits under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act cannot be treated as business income or qualify for deductions under Sections 80-IA/80-IB without proper proof of the creditors' identity and source. The decision emphasizes the need for taxpayers to maintain adequate documentation to substantiate such transactions. This ruling impacts tax compliance and business practices in India.

The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in India.
articles.raw_summary: Chief Justice Kant expressed that, “it may be time to rethink the very expression ‘Alternative Dispute Resolution’ since it is no longer an alternative to litigation”.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that an Assessing Officer (AO) cannot initiate fresh scrutiny after a company files a modified return following an amalgamation under Section 170A. The court held that the AO can only adjust income to reflect the reorganization if the assessment is already completed. This decision clarifies the scope of tax authorities' powers in post-amalgamation scenarios.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved whether fresh scrutiny could be initiated after filing a modified return under Section 170A. The amendment is expected to impact businesses engaged in intermediary services, including digital platforms and consultancy firms.

articles.title: Declaratory Decree Can't Be Set Aside Merely Because Plaintiff Didn't Seek Its Execution: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a declaratory decree (a court order establishing rights) cannot be set aside solely because the plaintiff did not seek its execution. The Court held that if the plaintiff is already in possession of the property, the execution of such a decree is immaterial. This decision clarifies the legal standing of declaratory decrees in property disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that execution of a decree declaring title is immaterial if the plaintiff is already in possession.

articles.title: Bank Deposits Treated as Business Turnover – ITAT Reduces Profit Rate from 10% to 2%
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that bank deposits linked to regular business activity cannot be treated as unexplained income. The tribunal reduced the profit rate from 10% to 2% for the assessee, citing consistent past records supporting their business receipts. This decision clarifies tax treatment for businesses with bank deposits as part of their turnover.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that bank deposits cannot be treated as unexplained income when linked to regular business activity. It upheld that consistent past records supported the assessee’s claim of business receipts. The case has significant implications for religious practices and constitutional rights in India.

articles.title: Judges Decide As Per Law But Are Called Independent Based On Outcomes In High-Stakes Cases: Justice Rajesh Bindal
articles.summary: Retiring Supreme Court judge Justice Rajesh Bindal stated that judges decide cases based on the law, but their independence is often judged by whether outcomes favor the government in high-stakes cases. He echoed former judge Ajay Rastogi's observation that judges are perceived as independent only when they rule against the government. The remarks were made during a farewell function, emphasizing the scrutiny judges face based on case outcomes rather than legal reasoning.

articles.title: Penalty Set Aside: Broker Not Liable Without Possession of Goods under Excise Rule 26(2)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a broker cannot be penalized under Excise Rule 26(2) if they merely acted as an intermediary without handling or possessing the goods. The tribunal set aside the penalty, stating that the absence of direct involvement or possession of goods made the rule inapplicable. This ruling clarifies the liability of brokers in excise-related transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that penalty cannot be imposed where the appellant merely acted as a broker without handling goods. Absence of possession or direct involvement made Rule 26 inapplicable. The post Penalty Set Aside: Broker Not Liable Without Possession of Goods under Excise Rule 26(2) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Immovable Property vs Plant & Machinery: AAAR Rejects ITC Claim in Inox Air Products Case
articles.summary: The Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAAR) rejected a GST Input Tax Credit (ITC) claim by Inox Air Products for leasehold rights used to construct a plant. The ruling held that ITC is blocked because the expense is linked to the construction of immovable property, with capitalization being the key determining factor. This decision impacts how businesses claim tax credits on capital expenditures for plant infrastructure under GST laws.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether ITC is available on GST paid for leasehold rights used to build a plant. The ruling held ITC is blocked as the expense is linked to construction of immovable property, with capitalisation being the key factor. The post Immovable Property vs Plant   Machinery: AAAR Rejects ITC Claim in Inox Air Products Case appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Income Tax Dept tightens disclosure norms under presumptive taxation scheme
articles.summary: The Income Tax Department has introduced stricter disclosure rules for taxpayers using the presumptive taxation scheme starting from the assessment year 2026-27. Taxpayers will now be required to disclose their bank balances and investments to prevent potential misuse of the scheme. The move aims to allow authorities to cross-verify reported income against financial assets, enhancing transparency and compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Starting from the assessment year 2026-27, a new mandate will compel taxpayers utilizing the presumptive taxation system to reveal their bank balances and investments. This initiative by the Income-Tax Department aims to curtail potential abuses of the scheme by allowing authorities to verify reported income against financial assets.

articles.title: Regulating Childhood
articles.summary: The death of three minor girls in Ghaziabad in 2026, linked to digital mediation, has sparked a policy debate in India. The incident underscores concerns about the increasing influence of the internet, social media, and algorithms on childhood development. The article, authored by Ishika Agarwal for Vidhi Centre for Legal Policy, examines how traditional influences like families and schools are being overshadowed by digital platforms. This raises urgent questions about child safety and regulator
articles.raw_summary: *Ishika Agarwal Introduction  Childhood is no longer shaped by families, schools, books, playgrounds or communities. After Covid-19 hit the world, it is increasingly mediated by the internet, social media and algorithms. In February 2026, the death of three minor girls in Ghaziabad sparked an urgent policy debate. The preliminary findings pointed towards a troubling mix [ ] The post Regulating Childhood appeared first on Vidhi Centre for Legal Policy .

articles.title: [Tenancy Act] Landlord's Right To Terminate Tenancy For 'Bona Fide Personal Cultivation' Extinguishes Upon Sale Of Land: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that a landlord's inherited right to terminate tenancy for bona fide personal cultivation extinguishes upon the sale of the land. The Court observed that once the land is sold, the requirement of personal cultivation ceases to exist, defeating the basis of such a claim. Justice Somasekhar Sundaresan was hearing writ petitions challenging a Maharashtra Revenue Tribunal order that had reversed the Collector's decision granting the petitioners entitlement to the dispu

articles.title: HNLU Organises Online Lecture To Mark 135th Birth Anniversary Of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar
articles.summary: Hidayatullah National Law University (HNLU) in Raipur organized an online lecture on April 14, 2026, to mark the 135th birth anniversary of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar. The event included a floral tribute to Ambedkar’s statue and was attended by the university’s Vice-Chancellor. The lecture focused on commemorating Ambedkar’s contributions to social justice and anti-discrimination, reflecting ongoing discussions around equality and rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: Hidayatullah National Law University, Raipur, through its Equal Opportunity & Anti-Discrimination Cell (EOADC) , organised an online lecture on “Commemorating the 135th Birth Anniversary of Bharat Ratna Dr. B. R. Ambedkar” on April 14, 2026. The programme commenced with a floral tribute ceremony at the statue of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar at the Academic Block, followed by the online lecture held from 10:30 AM to 11:15 AM. The programme was attended by Prof. (Dr.) V. C. Vivekanandan, Vice-Chancellor, HN

articles.title: Road ministry to charge up to four times the base fare from 40% overloaded vehicles on National Highways
articles.summary: The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways notified new National Highways Fee Rules to charge up to four times the base fare from vehicles overloaded by 40% or more on National Highways. The move aims to enforce permissible Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW) limits, enhance road safety, and protect highway infrastructure. The rules apply to overloaded vehicles beyond prescribed limits.
articles.raw_summary: The ministry of road transport and highways, Tuesday, notified the National Highways Fee Rules for fee collection from overloaded vehicles on National Highways beyond the permissible Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW), the move aimed at promoting compliance with prescribed load limits and enhancing road safety while protecting highway infrastructure.

articles.title: Life Lessons In Transfer Of Property Act, 1882
articles.summary: The Transfer of Property Act, 1882 is one of the most important legislations in the field of private law in India. Besides formulating the general principles of transfer governing both movable and immovable property, it provides five ways of transferring a property (in most cases, immovable) through sale, mortgage, lease, exchange and gift. articles.title: No Double Taxation of Benami Property – Protective Addition Deleted by ITAT
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that protective addition cannot be sustained in benami property cases if the income has already been taxed elsewhere. This decision prevents double taxation and clarifies tax enforcement rules. The ruling impacts how the Income Tax Department handles benami property disputes and tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether protective addition can survive when income is already taxed elsewhere. ITAT held that once income attains finality in another assessee’s case, protective addition cannot be sustained. The post No Double Taxation of Benami Property   Protective Addition Deleted by ITAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

Several other districts, including Raichur, Bidar, Vijayapura, Yadgir, and Bagalkot, also experienced temperatures between 42°C and 45°C. articles.title: Big Relief for Pigmy Agents: Karnataka HC Stops GST Reverse Charge on Pigmy Commission
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that pigmy agents are employees, not independent service providers, exempting their remuneration from GST under the reverse charge mechanism. This decision provides relief to pigmy agents by clarifying their employment status and tax obligations. The ruling directly impacts tax compliance and policy interpretation for similar workforce classifications in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether pigmy agents are independent service providers or employees. The Court held they are employees, so their remuneration is not a taxable supply under GST. The post Big Relief for Pigmy Agents: Karnataka HC Stops GST Reverse Charge on Pigmy Commission appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST Appeal Rejection Invalid as Bombay HC Allows Pre-Deposit Later to Ensure Merits Hearing
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that rejecting a GST appeal solely for non-payment of a pre-deposit is invalid. The court held that appellants must be given a chance to deposit the amount later to ensure their appeal is heard on merits. This decision impacts how GST appeals are processed and highlights the judiciary's role in ensuring fair tax dispute resolution.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was rejection of a GST appeal for non-payment of pre-deposit. The Court held that the appellant must be given an opportunity to deposit and have the appeal decided on merits. The post GST Appeal Rejection Invalid as Bombay HC Allows Pre-Deposit Later to Ensure Merits Hearing appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: 'Constitutional Morality' In Religious Matters Like A Bull In A China Shop : Singhvi Tells Supreme Court In Sabarimala Reference
articles.summary: Senior advocate Abhishek Singhvi argued before the Supreme Court that constitutional morality should govern governance, not religious rights, in the ongoing Sabarimala temple entry reference. The case involves the intersection of fundamental rights and religious practices, with the court examining the role of constitutional principles in such matters. Singhvi contended that constitutional morality is a code for governance, not a tool to test religious freedoms.
articles.raw_summary: Constitutional morality has been envisaged as a code for governance, and not to test religious rights, Singhvi argued.

articles.title: GST Appeal Rejection Set Aside for Ignoring Delay Condonation Grounds: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court set aside the rejection of a GST appeal by an appellate authority because it failed to consider the reasons provided for the delay. The court directed a fresh adjudication, emphasizing that orders cannot be based solely on procedural lapses without examining the merits. This ruling impacts tax dispute resolution processes under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The Court found that the appellate authority rejected the appeal solely on delay without examining the reasons provided. The case involves balancing religious customs with constitutional rights.

articles.title: Central government yet to notify selection panels for data protection board
articles.summary: Five months after establishing the Data Protection Board of India (DPBI), the government has yet to set up search-cum-selection committees to appoint its chairperson and four members. articles.title: ‘Electoral reform need of the day’: What plea before SC seeking biometric voting system says
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Election Commission, the central government, and state governments to respond on a proposal to introduce biometric voting at polling stations, using finger and iris identification. The court flagged the need to examine this electoral reform proposal. The SC's directive comes amid growing calls for enhancing the integrity and security of India's voting process. The responses will help determine whether such a system is implemented nationwide.

articles.title: Kejriwal moves fresh affidavit for recusal of Justice Swarana Sharma - ‘reasonable apprehension of conflict of interest’
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal has filed an affidavit requesting Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma to recuse herself from a case, citing a reasonable apprehension of conflict of interest. The concern arises because Justice Sharma's son and daughter are empanelled as Central Government Counsel, and the Solicitor General, who appeared for the CBI in a case before her, allocates work to them. The matter raises questions about judicial impartiality and transparency in the legal system.

articles.title: ITAT Allowed TDS Credit Despite Form 26AS Mismatch as Income Taxed in Same Year
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that taxpayers are entitled to Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) credit in the year the related income is assessed, even if the TDS is not reflected in Form 26AS. The decision clarifies that timing discrepancies by the deductor cannot deny taxpayers their rightful credit. This ruling impacts tax compliance and credit claims for individuals and businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that TDS credit must be granted in the year in which the related income is assessed, even if it is not reflected in Form 26AS. The case clarifies that timing differences by the deductor cannot deny rightful credit. The post ITAT Allowed TDS Credit Despite Form 26AS Mismatch as Income Taxed in Same Year appeared first on TaxGuru .

This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.

articles.title: India's trade gap shrinks in March, swells in FY26
articles.summary: India's trade deficit saw a reduction in March as the West Asia conflict disrupted trade flows. Exports and imports both declined, narrowing the monthly deficit. However, the overall trade deficit for the fiscal year widened. Despite challenges, India achieved record exports of $860 billion in FY26, showcasing resilience. Several free trade agreements are expected to boost future trade prospects.

Several free trade agreements are expected to boost future trade prospects.

articles.summary: The Indian government is reviewing its tax revenue targets for the fiscal year 2026-27 (FY27) due to rising crude oil prices, the West Asia crisis, and slower economic growth. The Centre is evaluating the impact of these external pressures on its revenue projections.

articles.summary: The Indian government has increased export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) to ₹55.5 per litre and ₹42 per litre, respectively. The hike is part of the windfall tax adjustments announced by the Centre.

The discussion is limited to the United States and does not involve Indian policies, regulations, or stakeholders.

articles.title: 11,000 litres of milk poured into Narmada after religious event; environmentalists see red
articles.summary: During a religious event in Madhya Pradesh, 1,000 litres of milk were poured into the Narmada River. Environmentalists warn this could deplete oxygen levels, pollute the water, and harm aquatic life. The incident highlights broader concerns about ritualistic pollution in rivers and may prompt calls for stricter environmental regulations or enforcement.

articles.title: UN Awaits Go-Ahead to Move Fertilizer Through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United Nations is preparing to establish a corridor to allow fertilizer shipments to pass freely through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route. This move depends on a political agreement to proceed. Fertilizer is essential for farmers during planting seasons, and disruptions in its supply could impact agricultural productivity worldwide, including India's food security.
articles.raw_summary: The United Nations is ready to set up a corridor to allow fertilizer to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz and reach farmers for the planting season — but doing so hinges on a political agreement to go forward, according to a top UN official.

articles.title: Fair Reporting Of FIR Not 'Media Trial'; Press Acts As Watchdog Of Democracy: Sikkim High Court
articles.summary: The Sikkim High Court has held that fair and accurate reporting of the registration of an FIR, without sensationalism or prejudgment, cannot be termed as a “media trial.”� It remarked that reporting on criminal proceedings is the role of the press as the fourth pillar of democracy and is a part of the media's duty as a public watchdog. The Court further clarified that such reportage, particularly when it does not disclose the identity of victims does not call for judicial restraint or sanctions.

articles.title: Deposition Of Hostile Witness Acceptable To Creditworthy Extent : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that courts cannot entirely dismiss the testimony of a hostile witness but must instead rely on the credible portions of their deposition. A bench of Justice Sanjay Kumar and Justice K. Vinod Chandran set aside an acquittal in a corruption case, emphasizing that only the 'creditworthy' parts of such evidence can be used to prove a case. The ruling clarifies judicial standards for evaluating hostile witness testimonies.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Wednesday (April 15) held that courts cannot discard the testimony of a hostile witness in its entirety and must instead identify and rely on the “creditworthy” portions of such evidence. “…every court considering the deposition of a hostile witness has to look at the extent of the deposition, which is creditworthy to provide proof, of the case set up.” , observed a bench of Justice Sanjay Kumar and Justice K. The situation may prompt policy responses to stabilize supply and prices for Indian farmers.

articles.title: Can TMC survive SIR disruptions and alliance wars in 2026
articles.summary: The article examines whether Mamata Banerjee’s Trinamool Congress (TMC) can sustain its political coalition in West Bengal amid administrative disruptions caused by the State Industrial Policy (SIR) and growing opposition alliances ahead of the 2026 elections. The analysis suggests potential implications for governance and electoral strategies in the state.

articles.title: US’ 301 probes: India denies allegations, seeks termination of both investigations

articles.title: Iran war impact: Essential drugs may cost up to 5% more, for now
articles.summary: The price of essential drugs in India may rise by 3-5% due to the ongoing Iran war, according to industry expectations. This increase is projected to last 3-4 months, with a possible rollback if input costs stabilize. The impact is similar to recent GST rate cuts, and the industry anticipates a temporary adjustment rather than a long-term change.
articles.raw_summary: The immediate net consumer impact could be 3-5% higher prices, or roughly similar to what consumers paid before the late-September cuts in GST rates. The industry expects the price increase to be in place for 3-4 months, with a rollback option once input costs stabilise.

articles.summary: A study over three decades reveals that exotic fish, invasive plants, and urban waste are disrupting the food web of the Veli-Akkulam estuary in Thiruvananthapuram. articles.summary: The Indian Air Force (IAF) will establish and operate its own depot facility for repairing and overhauling Tejas jet engines, with technical support from GE Aerospace. The facility aims to reduce turnaround time for engine maintenance. GE will provide training, spare parts, and specialized equipment, while the IAF will own and manage the facility. This move aligns with India's push for indigenous defence capabilities and reduced reliance on foreign maintenance support.

articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

The issue underscores broader challenges in workplace safety and gender-inclusive labour policies.

The blockade disrupts global oil supplies and could influence India's energy imports and diplomatic strategies.

The IEA also projects a sharp reversal in oil demand growth, with 2026 demand expected to turn negative. This could impact India's energy costs, inflation, and import strategies.

articles.title: Attention activism gains global momentum: Why it matters?
articles.summary: A global movement is growing where young people, particularly millennials and Gen Z, are pushing back against excessive screen time by organizing offline gatherings and promoting the use of 'dumb phones.' Activists aim to reduce the influence of Big Tech companies and encourage people to reconnect with real-life interactions. The movement is spreading across countries and highlights concerns about digital distraction and its societal impact.
articles.raw_summary: A new movement is emerging, with millennials and Gen Z pushing back against constant screen time. These groups are organising offline gatherings and promoting 'dumb phones' to reclaim their attention. Activists aim to reduce the influence of Big Tech companies. This rebellion against digital distraction is spreading across the globe, encouraging people to reconnect with real life and each other.

articles.title: Indian parents back social media ban for under-16s, new UK survey finds
articles.summary: Parents in India have strongly backed a social media ban for under-16s, logging the second-highest score at 75% after Malaysia (77%) in new worldwide research released by the UK-based education charity Varkey Foundation on Tuesday.
articles.raw_summary: Parents in India have strongly backed a social media ban for under-16s, logging the second-highest score at 75% after Malaysia (77%) in new worldwide research released by the UK-based education charity Varkey Foundation on Tuesday.

articles.title: In India, $1 housekeepers spark a consumer, worker frenzy despite safety risks
articles.summary: Indian startups are offering house help for as little as $1 an hour, attracting thousands of women seeking employment and consumers eager for affordable services. While the market is booming, concerns about worker safety, particularly for women, remain a significant challenge that companies are actively addressing through training and safety features.

articles.title: Govt's wheat procurement to drop 69% due to delayed arrivals
articles.summary: The Indian government's wheat procurement for the 2026-27 marketing year is expected to drop by 69% due to unseasonal rains delaying grain arrivals and affecting quality. This decline has led to a sharp reduction in current procurement compared to last year. The government has approved exports of some wheat and wheat products after assessing domestic supply and prices.
articles.raw_summary: Wheat procurement is set to drop significantly in the 2026-27 marketing year. Unseasonal rains delayed grain arrivals and affected quality. This has led to a sharp decline in current procurement figures compared to last year. The government has approved some wheat and wheat product exports after assessing domestic supply and prices.

articles.title: WPI inflation hits 38-month high of 3.9% in March as soaring energy, crude prices amid West Asia war drive up costs
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation reached a 38-month peak of 3.9% in March. This surge was fueled by rising costs of crude oil, energy, and manufactured goods. Experts anticipate further increases due to ongoing global tensions. Supply disruptions also contributed to higher input prices across various sectors. Retail inflation also saw a slight uptick.

articles.title: India achieves record exports of USD 860 billion in 2025-26: Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India has set a new export record, reaching $860 billion in shipments for the fiscal year 2025-26. This achievement surpasses the previous year's $825 billion. The nation's exports demonstrate resilience against global challenges. Nine new trade agreements are expected to boost this growth further, opening new markets and opportunities across various sectors.

articles.title: Geopolitical tensions cripple India-Middle East trade in March
articles.summary: India's trade with the Middle East saw a significant drop in March. Exports and imports fell by over 50 percent. This decline resulted from disruptions caused by the US-Israel attack on Iran. The Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, faced temporary closure. This impacted shipments of gems, jewellery, engineering goods, and rice.

articles.title: India‘s textile and apparel exports degrow by -2.22% in FY 26: Govt Data
articles.summary: Indian textile and apparel exports saw a decline in fiscal year 26. Exports in March also registered a year-on-year drop. This comes after the US imposed high tariffs. Meanwhile, India's import of raw cotton increased significantly. This surge followed a period of eased import duties.

However, exports in dollar value experienced a downturn amidst global market turbulence. To counter this, exporters expanded their reach, finding new opportunities in the UAE, Australia, and Canada.

articles.summary: India’s wholesale inflation rose to a 38-month high of about 3.9% in March from 2.1% in February, driven by higher prices of crude oil, power and manufactured goods amid the West Asia conflict.

articles.title: West Asia conflict: India's soybean meal exports fall 63%
articles.summary: India’s soybean meal exports dropped nearly 63% to 60,000 tonnes in March, hit by high domestic prices and disruptions from the West Asia conflict, according to Soybean Processors Association of India. Shipments to key markets like Iran and the UAE were affected. However, domestic demand stayed strong, with consumption rising to six lakh tonnes, supported by the animal feed industry.

articles.title: India must cut fertiliser import dependence, leverage AI and precision tech: ICAR chief
articles.summary: India is seeking self-reliance in fertilizers. Experts propose a multi-pronged approach using artificial intelligence and precision nutrient management. This strategy aims to reduce import dependence and optimize fertilizer use. Initiatives like the Soil Health Card scheme will be strengthened. A mission mode program will promote organic manures to replace mineral fertilizers.

articles.title: IMF raises India's growth forecast to 6.5% for FY27

articles.title: El Nino threat looms large as data shows strong link to weak monsoons
articles.summary: Around 70% of El Nino years since 1980 have been linked to weak Indian monsoons, underscoring a strong correlation as global agencies warn of a likely and potentially stronger event this summer. While most El Nino years have seen deficient rainfall, occasional offsets from factors like the Indian Ocean Dipole have helped cushion the impact, leaving the monsoon outlook contingent on how multiple climate forces evolve.

articles.title: Kharif season faces "double whammy" from El Nino risk and Gulf tensions: Experts
articles.summary: India's upcoming Kharif season faces a double challenge. A likely weak monsoon due to El Nino and ongoing Gulf tensions threaten farm output. Experts warn of rising food prices and reduced rural demand. While government measures may offer some relief, the situation remains uncertain. The monsoon's progress and easing of supply disruptions will be key.

articles.title: India's strong fundamentals to cushion oil shock, growth may slow up to 80 bps: S&P Global Ratings
articles.summary: India's strong economic and financial foundations are expected to absorb a sustained oil price shock, though growth could dip by 80 basis points if crude hits $130/barrel in 2026. Corporate earnings and bank asset quality might weaken, but robust balance sheets and well-capitalized banks offer significant buffers against prolonged disruptions.

This case relates to alleged corruption in the implementation of the Jal Jeevan Mission, a government scheme providing tap water connections to rural households.

articles.title: China moves out of factory deflation after 3 years as Iran war impacts producers
articles.summary: China has moved out of factory deflation for the first time in three years after the war in Iran drove up energy costs. Producer prices rose by 0.5% in March, though consumer inflation slowed and manufacturers' profits remained weak. The conflict in Iran has disrupted global energy supplies, which could have broader economic implications for countries like India that rely on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: China exits industrial deflation as war in Iran drives energy costs up, producer prices rise 0.5% in March, but consumer inflation slows and manufacturers profits stay weak

articles.title: TCS Nashik case: Nasscom stresses industry's zero tolerance on misconduct, calls incident isolated
articles.summary: TCS Nashik is facing allegations of sexual harassment and forced religious conversion, leading to police investigations and arrests. Nasscom, the industry body, has reiterated its zero-tolerance stance on misconduct, calling the incident isolated. Stricter workplace safeguards and probes are under review by authorities. The case raises concerns about corporate governance and employee welfare in India's IT sector.
articles.raw_summary: Nasscom backs zero tolerance as TCS Nashik faces sexual harassment and forced conversion claims, with probes, arrests and stricter safeguards under review.

Higher oil prices and geopolitical instability are cited as likely consequences.

articles.title: Oil prices steady after declining on moves to begin US-Iran talks again
articles.summary: Oil prices stabilized after a sharp decline on Tuesday, with Brent crude holding above $94 per barrel and West Texas Intermediate near $90. The price drop followed reports of renewed US-Iran negotiations, which could influence global oil supply. This development matters for India as it relies heavily on oil imports and faces inflation pressures from fuel costs.
articles.raw_summary: Brent steadied above $94 a barrel after declining nearly 5% on Tuesday. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate was near $90.

articles.title: Crude oil prices will soon reflect the extent of this energy supply crisis, IEA warns
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices dropped to $95 per barrel on Tuesday, and West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is testing $90 per barrel, down from earlier highs of $120. The decline follows hopes of resumed US-Iran talks, which could ease energy supply concerns. The International Energy Agency (IEA) warns that prices will soon reflect the severity of the global energy supply crisis. The situation may influence India's energy policies, including fuel pricing and import strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Brent crude prices fell to $95 a barrel on Tuesday, while the West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is testing levels of $90 a barrel on Wednesday during early Asian trading, significantly below the early-war highs of $120 a barrel, as the market is hopeful of more talks between the US and Iran, which would ultimately lead to a resolution.

articles.summary: S&P Global Energy warns that Brent crude oil prices could surge to $200 per barrel if the ongoing crisis in the Strait of Hormuz persists. The warning underscores potential risks to global oil markets and energy-dependent economies.

articles.title: Market volatility hits ULIP demand; insurance growth slows amid rate concerns: JM Financial
articles.summary: Life insurers in India are expected to report slower growth in Q4 due to weak March sales, offsetting earlier gains. Demand for Unit-Linked Insurance Plans (ULIPs) declined because of market volatility, while customers delayed purchases over interest rate concerns. HDFC Life and ICICI Prudential may miss growth targets, while SBI Life and Axis Max Life are expected to perform better. Early April trends remain soft, though seasonal factors may be influencing the slowdown.

HDFC Life and ICICI Prudential may miss growth targets, while SBI Life and Axis Max Life are expected to perform better. Early April trends remain soft, though seasonal factors may be at play.

articles.title: European Gas futures jump up to 18% on Strait of Hormuz concerns
articles.summary: European gas futures surged up to 18% due to concerns over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy shipments. The strait carries nearly 20% of the world's energy supply, including liquefied natural gas (LNG), though no LNG shipments have passed through it yet. The situation highlights potential risks to energy markets and supply chains, which could impact India's energy imports and pricing.
articles.raw_summary: The Strait of Hormuz carries nearly 20% of the global energy supply, including LNG. While some oil supertankers traversed the strait over the weekend, LNG shipments haven’t yet made the journey.

articles.title: Crude oil prices surge, back above $100 after Trump threats on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The US Central Command announced it will block ships entering or exiting the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday at 10 AM Eastern Time, except those heading to non-Iranian ports. The move follows threats by former US President Trump and could disrupt global oil supplies. Crude oil prices have already surged back above $100 per barrel in response. India, a major oil importer, may face energy price volatility and supply chain disruptions as a result.
articles.raw_summary: US Central Command has said that they will begin blocking ships entering and exiting the Strait of Hormuz from Monday 10 AM Eastern Time, but ships headed to non-Iranian ports will not be blocked.
